title: approvals — authorization policies
description: Four policies gate the module's write paths. Approvable-action management is platform-only (Academorix defines the registry — tenants don't add new action types). Template CRUD is tenant-admin. Instance abilities are per-role: viewAny + view + withdraw for requesters + admins, approve + reject for approvers only (with a deeper defense — the caller MUST be in the requirement's resolved_approvers). Decisions are effectively write-once — no update, no delete.

abilities.scope: tenant plane: any authenticated user in the tenant can list approvable-actions their tenant has active templates on (used by requester UI to explain 'why did this need approval?'). platform plane: platform_admin only.
same as viewAny
platform_admin only — usually the seeder does the writes; manual creation is a break-glass for actions registered outside the boot-time discovery pass.
platform_admin only — mostly toggles is_active.
tenant admin + owner
tenant admin + owner
tenant admin + owner + entitlement approval_templates_custom (small-tier tenants can't author custom templates)
tenant admin + owner — triggers version-bump on immutable-field changes
tenant admin + owner — deactivates the peer active row per one-is-active invariant
tenant admin + owner
tenant admin + owner + no active instances (Observer refuses)
same as create — preflight is a dry-run against an untouched DB, but authors need the same authorship permission to test their expression syntax
platform_admin cross-tenant
any authenticated tenant user — the listing endpoint already scopes to `requester_id=caller OR caller IN resolved_approvers`
requester + any approver (in any requirement's resolved_approvers, past or present) + tenant admin
requester ONLY, when status='pending' AND template.allows_recall=true — admin cannot withdraw on the requester's behalf (that would be a governance bypass; use reject with a comment instead)

caller MUST be in requirement.resolved_approvers AND requirement.status='pending' AND caller has NOT already decided on this requirement. Enforced by ApprovalDecisionObserver AND caller_is_approver validation rule AND approvals.enforce_approver middleware (defense-in-depth: three layers, so a policy misconfiguration doesn't open a forgery seam).
same as approve (uses the same permission — approve/reject are two sides of the same decision-authority)
platform_admin cross-tenant
platform_admin cross-tenant
requester + any current-or-past approver on the parent requirement + tenant admin. Comment visibility gated separately by approvals.decisions.view_comments.

abilities.note: No `update`, `create`, `delete` — decisions are recorded via the parent instance's approve/reject endpoints AND are write-once. Any attempt to mutate a decision row fires ApprovalSecurityViolation.
abilities.scope: REFUSED — direct decision writes not supported by any endpoint
